description: A high-power rocket requires a structural overhaul to survive the extreme shear forces of a Level 2 certification flight. Modify the existing surface-mounted fins of dual_parachute_deployment.ork into a robust Through-The-Wall (TTW) configuration with a tab length of at least 100 mm and a tab height of at least 20 mm. Add two plywood centering rings named 'Forward Fin Ring' and 'Aft Fin Ring' to the booster body tube to internally brace the fin tabs. Upgrade the propulsion system by assigning an AeroTech J350W motor and run a flight simulation to verify structural limits. Save the modified design to /home/ga/Documents/rockets/ttw_fin_upgrade.ork. Write a structural upgrade report to /home/ga/Documents/exports/ttw_upgrade_report.txt containing the fin tab dimensions, confirmation of the plywood centering rings, the assigned motor, and the simulated Maximum Velocity and Maximum Acceleration.
